description:
Flew south to north at a height of about 40 feet almost directly over my head as I walked along road in C Loop of campground (I stopped when I saw the bird!). At first assumed it wouild be Turkey Vulture because it had the general shape and size of TV. But then noticed a couple of key points that distinguished it. Long and
relatively narrow winged blackish buteo with rather long somewhat narrow tail. Holding wings in a dihedral. Larger head than Turkey Vulture and dark in color not red, protruding significantly in front of the remainder of the body, much more so than in the vulture. Mostly black tail, with a notable white band in the middle of the tail and at least one narrower white band closer to the base (as seen from below). Remiges paler than blackish wing lingings, more gray than black, as seen from below.
